title: NBA Daily Digest
subtitle: May 17, 2026: Game 7 Showdown, WCF Set, MVP Race Heats Up!

items.headline: OG Anunoby Cleared for Eastern Conference Finals!
items.summary: Knicks star forward OG Anunoby is a full participant in practice and is expected to be ready for the Eastern Conference Finals beginning Tuesday. He sustained a right hamstring injury in Game 2 on May 6 against the 76ers, missing the remainder of the series, but it's reportedly less severe than past injuries.

items.headline: NBA MVP to be Announced Today: SGA, Wembanyama, Jokic Finalists
items.summary: The highly anticipated NBA MVP award winner will be revealed today (Sunday, May 17, 2026). Reigning MVP Shai Gilgeous-Alexander (OKC) is among the three finalists, joined by San Antonio's Victor Wembanyama and Denver's Nikola Jokic, setting the stage for a thrilling announcement.

items.headline: Spurs Dominate Wolves in Game 6, Advance to Western Conference Finals
items.summary: The San Antonio Spurs sealed their spot in the Western Conference Finals with a decisive 139-109 victory over the Minnesota Timberwolves in Game 6 on Friday night, closing out the series 4-2. They will face the top-seeded Oklahoma City Thunder.

items.headline: Pistons Force Game 7 with Dominant Game 6 Win!
items.summary: The Detroit Pistons, led by Cade Cunningham's stellar play, defeated the Cleveland Cavaliers 115-94 in Game 6 on Friday night, evening their Eastern Conference Semifinals series at 3-3 and forcing a decisive Game 7.

items.headline: Spurs Eliminate Timberwolves, Head to Western Conference Finals!
items.summary: The San Antonio Spurs blew out the Minnesota Timberwolves 139-109 in Game 6, securing their trip to the Western Conference Finals where they will face the Oklahoma City Thunder. Stephon Castle led the charge with a monster performance.

items.headline: Eastern Conference Semifinals: Game 7 Showdown!
items.summary: It all comes down to this! The Detroit Pistons host the Cleveland Cavaliers in a winner-take-all Game 7 of the Eastern Conference Semifinals at Little Caesars Arena. The victor advances to face the New York Knicks in the Eastern Conference Finals.

items.headline: Stephon Castle's Dominant Game 6
items.statContext: Led SAS in elimination win over MIN.

items.headline: Cade Cunningham Forces Game 7
items.statContext: Key to DET's Game 6 victory over CLE.

items.headline: Jalen Duren's Double-Double Impact
items.statContext: Crucial performance for Pistons in Game 6 win.

items.headline: Paul Reed's Bench Spark
items.statContext: Efficient scoring off the bench for DET in Game 6.

items.headline: Deni Avdija's Strong Season & Playoff Flashes
items.statContext: Israeli forward for POR. Notable playoff performances include a 41-point, 12-assist, 7-rebound Play-In game and a 22-point, 3-assist, 3-rebound game in the First Round against SAS.

items.headline: Thunder vs. Spurs: Western Conference Finals Set to Tip Off Monday
items.summary: The reigning champion Oklahoma City Thunder, fresh off a sweep of the Lakers, will begin their Western Conference Finals series against the San Antonio Spurs on Monday. The Spurs advanced by eliminating the Timberwolves in Game 6.

items.headline: Knicks Await Eastern Conference Finals Opponent
items.summary: The New York Knicks, after sweeping the Philadelphia 76ers in the second round, are awaiting the winner of today's Game 7 between the Detroit Pistons and the Cleveland Cavaliers to determine their Eastern Conference Finals opponent.

items.headline: Game 7 Injury Report: Key Players Questionable for Pistons, Cavs
items.summary: For the decisive Game 7, the Pistons have Kevin Huerter (left adductor strain), Caris LeVert (right heel contusion), and Duncan Robinson (low back soreness) listed as questionable. The Cavaliers' Larry Nance Jr. is doubtful due to illness.

items.headline: Warriors Hopeful to Retain Porzingis and Horford
items.summary: The Golden State Warriors are reportedly optimistic about bringing back Kristaps Porzingis and Al Horford next season. Porzingis, an unrestricted free agent, is expected to begin contract talks soon.

items.headline: Pistons Eyeing Michael Porter Jr. in Offseason?
items.summary: Rumors suggest the Detroit Pistons could pursue Brooklyn Nets forward Michael Porter Jr. this offseason, especially depending on the outcome of their Game 7 clash with the Cavaliers.

items.headline: Donovan Mitchell's Future in Cleveland Hinges on Game 7
items.summary: Cleveland Cavaliers star Donovan Mitchell could become a prominent name in the NBA trade rumor mill if the Cavaliers fail to advance past Game 7 of the Eastern Conference Semifinals.
